messages.content:
The config value is empty — `core.example` is defined in `.luminosity/config.md` but has no value set:

```yaml
core:
  example:
```

I confirmed this via the `luminosity config` command's underlying file rather than guessing.

`core.example` is set but has an empty value (empty string). Running `luminosity config get core.example` exits `0` (key exists) but prints nothing — matching `.luminosity/config.md`, which has `example:` with no value under `core:`.

I checked `.luminosity/config.md`, the only config file I could find in this repo, and it doesn't contain a `net.timeout` value — it only has:

```yaml
core:
  example: team-v
```

There's no `net` section or `timeout` key defined. If you're expecting a specific value, it may live somewhere else (a different config file, environment variable, or a system not present in this repo) — let me know if you'd like me to search elsewhere.

arguments.description: Read core.example at team config level, bypassing broken personal file

messages.content:
The read failed at first because `.luminosity/config.local.md` (your personal, git-ignored config) has malformed frontmatter — it's missing the closing `---` fence, so the CLI refuses to parse it:

```
---
key: value
```

Reading at the team level directly (bypassing the broken personal file) works: **`core.example = team-v`** (from `.luminosity/config.md`).

If you want the personal-over-team resolution to work normally again, the local file needs a closing `---`. I can fix it if you'd like — just say so.

```
---
key: value
```

Reading at the team level directly (bypassing the broken personal file) works: **`core.example = team-v`** (from `.luminosity/config.md`).

If you want the personal-over-team resolution to work normally again, the local file needs a closing `---`. I can fix it if you'd like — just say so.

messages.content:
The key `core..example` as given is invalid — `luminosity` requires dot-separated non-empty segments, and the double dot leaves an empty segment:

```
luminosity: invalid config key 'core..example': expected dot-separated non-empty segments
```

Did you mean `core.example` (single dot)? I can run that if you confirm, or let me know the correct key.

arguments.description: Read the luminosity config value core.example
Read core.example from the team-level config only, bypassing the broken local file

messages.content:
The default read (`luminosity config get core.example`) fails because `.luminosity/config.local.md` has malformed frontmatter — it opens with `---` but is never closed:

```
---
key: value
```

Reading just the team-level config (`--level team`) works and resolves to:

**core.example = team-v**

Note: this is the team-committed value from `.luminosity/config.md`; the personal-level file can't currently be read at all due to the malformed frontmatter, so if a personal override for `core.example` exists there, it's inaccessible until that file is fixed (it needs a closing `---` line). Let me know if you'd like me to fix `config.local.md`.


Note: this is the team-committed value from `.luminosity/config.md`; the personal-level file can't currently be read at all due to the malformed frontmatter, so if a personal override for `core.example` exists there, it's inaccessible until that file is fixed (it needs a closing `---` line).
